info.title: Piwi Dashboard API
info.description: REST API for storing and querying Playwright test results, traces, failure diagnoses, and project statistics.

delete.description: Deletes test runs older than a specified number of days, including associated files, traces, and reports. Optionally runs a full VACUUM (SQLite) to return freed space to the filesystem. Requires administrator role.

olderThanDays.description: Delete runs older than this many days

vacuum.description: Run a blocking full VACUUM after cleanup (SQLite only; ignored on PostgreSQL)

get.description: Returns aggregate statistics about projects, test runs, test cases, files, and storage disk usage. Requires administrator role.
Returns public AI configuration status including whether AI diagnosis is configured, the provider, model, and auto-diagnose setting.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Period length in days (the period ends now)

parameters.name: projects
parameters.description: Comma-separated project ids to restrict to

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: Restrict to runs reported for one environment

get.description: Returns the data for one analytics widget (see the widget registry: insights, portfolio, pass-rate-heatmap, ci-time-trend, wasted-time, flaky-leaderboard, cluster-landscape), aggregated across every project the caller can see.

post.description: Allows an authenticated user to change their own password by verifying their current password first.
If a non-OAuth user with the given email exists, sends a password reset link. Always returns 200 to prevent user enumeration. Rate-limited.
Authenticates a user with username and password credentials and creates a session.
Clears the current user session and logs the user out.

get.description: Returns the currently authenticated user details or unauthenticated status.
Handles the OAuth provider callback, exchanges the authorization code, creates or links the user, and sets the session.
Redirects the user to the configured OAuth provider for authentication. Pass `?link=1` while signed in to connect the provider to the current account instead of signing in.

post.description: Removes the OAuth provider link from the current user. Requires the account to have a password set so the user keeps a way to sign in.
Validates a single-use reset/invite token, sets the new password, and invalidates existing sessions.
Sends a verification link to the authenticated user's email address.

get.description: Reports whether the first-admin setup form should be shown — true only when auth is enabled and the users table is empty.

post.description: Creates the first administrator user. Only available when no users exist yet. Accepts username, password, and optional name in the request body.

get.description: Validates a verify token from the emailed link and marks the account email as verified.

delete.description: Deletes a channel and all associated subscriptions and deliveries.

post.description: Sends a test notification through the specified channel.

get.description: Returns channels owned by the current user and global (admin-managed) channels. Auto-creates a personal email channel if the user has an account email set.

post.description: Creates a new notification channel. Webhook secrets are encrypted at rest.
Merges the two suggested clusters (lower id survives) and consumes the suggestion. Requires reporter or administrator role.
Marks the suggestion as rejected; both clusters are left untouched. Requires reporter or administrator role.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a failure cluster including affected tests, last seen run status, project info, and diagnosis.

patch.summary: Update manual base commit for a cluster
patch.description: Persists a manual baseline commit SHA for a failure cluster used in AI diagnosis context.

get.summary: List branches for a cluster repository
Get commit diff for a cluster
get.description: Returns the file changes and patches for a specific commit SHA in the cluster repository.

get.summary: List recent commits for a cluster
get.description: Returns recent commits for the failure cluster repository. Supports optional baseline query parameter for aggregate diff stats.
Returns a preview of the full AI context that would be sent for diagnosis. Supports ?format=json for a structured response with per-section breakdown, token estimate, and coverage metadata.

post.summary: Run AI diagnosis for a cluster
post.description: Triggers an AI-powered diagnosis for the specified failure cluster. Accepts optional force flag, additional context, images, base commit, and selected commit SHAs in the request body.

post.summary: Run AI diagnosis with streaming response
post.description: Triggers an AI-powered diagnosis for the specified failure cluster and returns the result as a Server-Sent Events stream. Text tokens are pushed as `event: thinking` chunks; the final structured result arrives as `event: result`.

get.summary: Get diagnosis history for a cluster
get.description: Returns previous diagnosis versions for a failure cluster, ordered by creation date descending.

get.summary: Get stored diagnosis for a cluster
get.description: Returns the stored AI diagnosis result and manual base commit for a failure cluster.

post.summary: Extract test cases from failure cluster
post.description: Unlinks selected test cases from a failure cluster by setting their failureClusterId to NULL. Optionally updates the triage note.

patch.description: Updates the status (open, resolved, ignored) and optional triage note for a failure cluster.
Record thumbs up/down feedback on a diagnosis result, with an optional note.

get.description: Serves stored files including test reports, trace archives, and attachments. Supports trace ZIP reconstruction from slim blobs and gzip decompression for report archives.

Liveness/readiness probe for containers, load balancers, and uptime monitors. Verifies the database is reachable with a lightweight query. Returns 200 when healthy, 503 when the database cannot be queried. Public.

delete.description: Remove an entity link by ID.

patch.description: Update the URL and/or title of an entity link. Provider is re-detected if the URL changes.

post.description: Re-run provider detection, key extraction, and unfurl (fetch title) for a link.

get.description: Returns all entity links attached to a run, test-case run, or test case.

post.description: Attach an external URL to a run, test-case run, or test case. Provider is auto-detected from the URL.

delete.description: Deletes a project timeline marker. Requires reporter or administrator role.

put.description: Updates a project timeline marker. Requires reporter or administrator role.

get.description: Returns all projects with their latest run, total runs count, total test cases, and tags

post.description: Creates a project with optional label, description, and tag associations. Requires administrator role.

delete.description: Permanently delete a project and all its associated data including test runs, reports, traces, failure clusters, and test cases. Administrator access required.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of recent runs to include

put.description: Updates project metadata including label, description, diagnosis instructions, SCM token, and tags. Requires administrator role.

get.summary: List cluster merge suggestions for a project
get.description: Returns pending (or filtered) merge suggestions surfaced by the embedding reconciler / LLM adjudicator, each joined with both clusters’ summaries.

get.summary: List failure clusters for a project
get.description: Returns failure clusters grouped by error fingerprint with occurrence counts, affected tests count, and compact diagnosis info. Supports optional status filter.

post.description: Analyzes a flaky test case and classifies its root cause as timing, network, assertion, environment, or other

get.description: Analyzes test flakiness across recent runs using retry-pass detection and pass/fail alternation scoring. Pass an environment to scope the analysis to runs from that deployment environment.

get.summary: Get latest run info for a project
get.description: Returns the id and status of the most recent test run for the project
Returns the dated timeline markers (deploys, config changes, incidents, ...) for a project.

post.description: Creates a dated timeline marker for a project. Requires reporter or administrator role.

get.description: Returns all users who have access to this project, including those with explicit assignment, global access, and administrators (implicit access).

put.description: Replaces the explicit (non-global) user assignments for this project. Does not affect users with global access. Requires administrator role.

get.description: Returns test run duration, average test duration, and p90 test duration for trend charts with optional date range filtering
Returns the slowest test cases for a project with average, max, min duration, run count, and trend direction
Groups test cases by spec file prefix and computes pass rate, flaky rate, failure count, test count, and average duration over the last N days

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on title or file path

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Comma-separated status categories to include: passed, failed, flaky, skipped, didnotrun

parameters.name: maxAgeDays
parameters.description: Only include cases executed within the last N days (0 = all time)

get.summary: List test cases for a project with aggregated stats
get.description: Paginated test-case catalog with per-case aggregates: total runs, pass/fail/skip/flaky counts, executed-only pass rate and average duration, derived status category, and last run. Returns `{ items, total, limit, offset }`. Timed-out runs are folded into the failed counts.
Returns a slim list of all projects (id, name, label) ordered by last update. Used by the sidebar menu — much lighter than GET /api/projects.
Returns a compact overview per project: last 20 full runs (for trend bars), tendency badge, and key stats. Used by the home page dashboard.

get.summary: Search across projects, test runs, and test cases
get.description: Full-text search across project names/labels, run labels/IDs, and test case titles. Returns up to 5 results per category.

Returns full AI configuration: per-role provider settings (diagnosis, research, embedding), API key presence, auto-diagnose toggle, custom instructions, and SCM token presence. Requires administrator role.

put.description: Updates the per-role AI configuration (diagnosis, research, embedding), auto-diagnose toggle, custom instructions, and SCM token. Each role has its own provider/model/baseUrl/apiKey, or `reuse` to inherit another role. Requires administrator role. Not available when AI is managed via environment variables.

get.description: Returns the effective AI diagnosis context limits (defaults ← stored settings ← env vars), their defaults, the keys pinned by environment variables, and field metadata. Requires administrator role.

put.description: Persists overrides for the AI diagnosis context limits. Values are clamped to each field range; an empty/null value resets a field to its default. Fields pinned by environment variables are ignored. Requires administrator role.

post.summary: List available models from an AI provider
post.description: Calls the provider's models endpoint to return available models with metadata. Accepts provider, baseUrl, apiKey, and role in the request body; when apiKey is omitted, the saved key for that role (then the diagnosis role) is used. Requires administrator role.

Sends a connectivity test to the configured AI provider for a given model role (`diagnosis`, `research`, or `embedding` — the embedding role is probed via the embeddings endpoint). Accepts optional role, provider, apiKey, model, and baseUrl in the request body; omitted fields fall back to the saved configuration. Requires administrator role.

get.description: Aggregates AI diagnosis token usage over the requested period, grouped by provider and model. Requires administrator role.
Returns SMTP configuration display info (host, port, from address, configured status). Password is never returned. Requires administrator role.

post.description: Sends a test email via the env-configured SMTP settings to verify the configuration. Requires administrator role.

get.description: Returns the allowlist of glob patterns that classify wait steps as wasted time, whether it is managed by the PIWI_WASTED_WAIT_PATTERNS environment variable, and the built-in defaults. Patterns match a wait step title or its source location. Requires administrator role.

put.description: Updates the allowlist of glob patterns used to classify wait steps as wasted time. Send `patterns: null` to reset to the built-in defaults. Wasted time is recomputed at read time, so changes apply to historical runs immediately. Not available when managed via PIWI_WASTED_WAIT_PATTERNS. Requires administrator role.

get.description: Subscribes to global run events (status changes, case updates) over SSE

patch.description: Updates events, filters, mode, muting, or active state.

get.description: Returns the current user's subscriptions (admins can see all).

post.description: Creates a new subscription for the current user.

delete.description: Deletes a tag by ID. Requires administrator role.

put.description: Updates the text and/or color of an existing tag. Requires administrator role.

get.description: Returns a list of all tags ordered alphabetically.

post.description: Creates a new tag with text (max 50 characters) and color. Requires administrator role.

get.description: Returns the stable test case identity with aggregated run stats, recent executions, linked failure clusters, and entity links.

get.summary: Get execution history for a test case
get.description: Returns the execution history across multiple test runs for a stable test case, ordered by most recent first. Accepts a test_case.id directly.

get.summary: Stability trend for a test case
get.description: Returns time-series of flaky rate, pass rate, avg duration grouped into N buckets for a single test case
Returns detailed information about a test run case (one execution in a test run) including test run data, failure cluster context, reports, and attachments.

post.summary: Run AI diagnosis for a test run case
post.description: Triggers an AI-powered diagnosis for the specified test run case (execution scope). Uses its failure cluster for context if available.

get.description: Returns a preview of the full AI context that would be sent for diagnosing a specific test-run-case. Supports ?format=json for a structured response with per-section breakdown.

get.summary: Get stored execution-scoped diagnosis for a test run case
get.description: Returns the stored execution-scoped AI diagnosis result for a single test-run case, if one exists.

get.summary: Get trace files for a test run case
get.description: Returns a list of trace files associated with a specific test run case (one execution).

delete.description: Permanently delete a test run and all associated data including reports, traces, files, and failure clusters. Administrator access required.

get.description: Returns full details for a specific test run, including project info, attached reports, test cases with results, and storage statistics.

patch.description: Updates a test run's metadata (label). Requires any authenticated user.

post.summary: Transition test run from initialising to running
post.description: Begins a streaming test run by transitioning it from "initialising" to "running" status. Requires the setup token returned by the setup endpoint. Supports sharded runs: already-running sharded runs accept additional setup tokens.

post.summary: Upload trace and attachment files for a test case
post.description: Upload trace files and attachments for a specific test case during an active streaming run. Authenticated by the run stream token. Supports trace deduplication via SHA-256 hashing and is idempotent on retry.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Which representation to render — trace-derived DOM (default) or the ARIA tree

get.summary: Render the failure-time DOM snapshot for a test-run case
get.description: Extracts the DOM snapshot from the stored trace ZIP, falling back to a nested render of the captured ARIA snapshot when no trace is available. Pass `source=aria` to render the ARIA tree even when a trace exists. Input values, inline handlers and script bodies are never included; token-shaped strings are masked.

get.summary: Diff a test case execution environment against its last pass
get.description: Compares whitelisted run/browser environment keys (Playwright version, browser config, locale, viewport, CI provider, …) of this execution against the same test case last passing execution on the same browser. Returns only the changed keys; an empty list means the environment is identical.

get.summary: Get locator healing suggestions for a failed test case
get.description: Returns ranked alternative locator suggestions for a failing locator in a test run case. Uses pre-captured element snapshots from the last passing run (including snapshots captured by other tests in the project that use the same locator), falling back to ARIA snapshot analysis.

post.summary: Save a user-picked locator for a failing test case
post.description: Saves a locator picked by a user from the interactive DOM snapshot picker. The pick is keyed to the failing locator call site (location and signature re-derived server-side from the stored error) and appears first in subsequent locator-healing responses. Any authenticated project member may save a pick.

failingLocator.description: The failing locator as displayed in the panel — identity fallback when the stored error cannot be re-parsed.

element.description: The picked element as probed in the DOM snapshot: tagName, attributes, textContent, accessibleName, center.

parameters.name: sha1
parameters.description: Content hash of the body resource, with or without its file extension

get.summary: Preview one request/response body stored in the trace
get.description: Serves a capped preview of a body resource referenced by the trace's network stream, addressed by its content hash (`sha1` from the trace-network response). Only hashes the trace itself references resolve. JSON is pretty-printed, token-shaped strings are masked, images return as a data URI; oversized or binary bodies report `too-large` / `unsupported`.

get.summary: Full network activity recorded in the stored trace
get.description: Parses the trace ZIP's HAR-like network stream into every request the page made — all resource types with status, sizes, timing phases, a relative waterfall timeline and the failing action's time window. Sensitive header values are masked and never returned; response bodies are fetched separately via the trace-network-body endpoint. Returns `status: no-trace` / `empty` (HTTP 200) when the data is absent.

get.summary: Full call stack of the failing action from the stored trace
get.description: Parses the trace ZIP's stacks index and embedded source files into the complete call stack of the failing action — every frame with file:line, function name and a source window when the trace was recorded with sources. Returns `status: no-trace` / `no-stacks` (HTTP 200) when the data is absent; traces recorded without sources degrade to frames with `source: null`.

get.summary: Pixel-diff a failing screenshot against the last passing run
get.description: Compares the failing execution screenshot with the same test case last passing execution screenshot (same browser). Computed lazily on first request and cached as a stored overlay artifact with changed-pixel metrics. Screenshots with different dimensions are compared on a padded union canvas and flagged dimensionMismatch.

post.summary: Submit test case events for a streaming run
post.description: Submit test case begin and complete events for an active streaming test run. Requires the stream token. Supports both single and batch event submission for real-time progress updates.

get.summary: Get failure groups for a test run
get.description: Returns clustered failure groups for a test run, grouping failed test cases by their failure cluster. Includes compact diagnosis data, flakiness detection, and worker correlation analysis.

post.description: Finalize a streaming test run by setting its final status and calculating performance metrics. Supports pending uploads mode where reports are uploaded asynchronously after finishing. For sharded runs, counters are accumulated and the run finishes only after all shards report.

post.summary: Keep a streaming test run alive

get.description: Returns comparison insights for a test run: new regressions, recurrences, recovered tests, performance changes, worker imbalance, and new clusters
Returns aggregated network request summaries from test cases in a test run, grouped by HTTP method and normalized route, sorted by average duration. Excludes static asset requests.

get.summary: Get regression context for a test run
get.description: Returns regression analysis context for a test run, comparing its failures against historical test data from the same project.

get.summary: Stream test run events via SSE
get.description: Subscribe to Server-Sent Events for a live test run. Sends an initial catch-up snapshot of current state and existing test cases, then streams real-time events (test-begin, test-completed, run-progress, run-finished) until the run completes.
Returns a lightweight summary of a test run including run metadata and its test cases with titles, statuses, durations, and locations.
Returns the 30 most recent completed test runs across all projects plus any currently active runs, sorted by start time. Used by the home page dashboard.

post.summary: Initialize a streaming test run in setup phase
post.description: Initialize a new streaming test run in "initialising" status. Returns a setup token to be used by the begin endpoint to transition the run to "running" status. Cancels any previous runs from the same instance. Supports sharded runs.

Start a new streaming test run directly in "running" status. Returns a stream token for authenticating subsequent streaming event submissions. Cancels any previous runs from the same instance. Supports sharded runs: when shardTotal > 1, reuses an existing run from the same instanceId.
Submit Playwright test run results as a JSON payload. Creates or updates a project, test run, and test cases. Supports sharded runs via shardIndex / shardTotal.

post.summary: Upload test results with reports, traces, and attachments
post.description: Upload Playwright test run results as multipart form data, including HTML reports, trace files, and other attachments. Supports both new runs and attaching files to existing streaming runs.

delete.description: Deletes all test projects and test tags by known names. Only available in non-production environments with administrator role.

post.description: Checks which trace blob SHA-256 hashes already exist in storage for a given project. Accepts projectName and hashes array in the request body.

delete.description: Deletes a user by ID. Prevents self-deletion and removal of the last administrator account. Requires administrator role.

patch.description: Updates a user's name, email, or role. Admins can update any user; non-admins can only update their own name and email.

delete.description: Revokes an API key by ID. Non-administrators can only revoke their own keys.

get.summary: List API keys for a user
get.description: Returns API keys belonging to a specific user. Non-administrators can only list their own keys.

post.summary: Create an API key for a user
post.description: Creates a new API key for a user. The plaintext key is returned once and cannot be retrieved again. Accepts name and optional expiresAt in the request body. Non-administrators can only create keys for themselves.
Sends an invite email to a user with a link to set their password. Requires the user to have an email address set. Requires administrator role.

get.description: Returns the project assignments for a user, including whether they have global access and a list of explicit project IDs. Administrators always have implicit access to all projects.

put.description: Sets the project assignments for a user. If global is true, the user gets access to all projects. If global is false, the user gets access only to the specified project IDs. Cannot set assignments for administrators.

get.description: Returns a list of all users (password fields excluded). Requires authentication.

post.description: Creates a new user with username, password, role (administrator, reporter, or user), and optional name. Requires administrator role.

get.summary: Get server version and runtime info
get.description: Returns the dashboard application version, build provenance, and live runtime info (Node version, database backend). Public — used by the Settings → About page and external monitors.
